title: Europe heat wave: 1,000 excess deaths recorded in France
summary: The French Health Ministry noted a sharp increase in deaths especially in areas hit hardest by the heat wave. Germany is meanwhile breaking for another potentially record-breaking day. Follow DW for the latest.

title: Germany news: Heat record broken for second consecutive day
summary: As Europe's heatwave rages, Germany breaks all-time temperature record for the second day in a row. Elsewhere, politicians bicker over reforms and industry blames train operator DB for billions in losses.

title: Germany news: Berlin's orchestra eases dress code amid heat
summary: Men are not obliged to wear jackets during the orchestra's end-of-season performance this weekend. Meanwhile, a court has sentenced the defendant in the Magdeburg Christmas market attack to life.

title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
summary: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
summary: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.
